truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 identified 6 of the 20 true vulnerabilities (SC-V3-001, SC-V3-004, SC-V3-005, SC-V3-006, SC-V3-009, SC-V3-010) with exact evidence quotes, but missed 14 others. All found vulnerabilities were supported by the source code; however, Run 2 consistently underclaimed severity (marking Critical issues as High or Medium) and occasionally misclassified CWEs (e.g., CWE-120 vs CWE-122). No false positive findings were present.

Items.Notes: Run 2 explicitly identified a format string vulnerability in logging with CWE-134 and quoted the exact printf call from the source.
Run 2 did not mention integer overflow in the factorial computation loop.
Run 2 did not identify the use-after-free condition where deferred history flush threads access memory pool allocations after cleanup.
Run 2 identified OS command injection in config validation with CWE-78 and quoted the vulnerable system call.
Run 2 identified buffer overflow in input processing and quoted the exact vulnerable strcpy check.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the race condition in the thread-safe counter with CWE-362 and quoted the read-modify-write occurring outside the lock.
Run 2 did not identify the logic bomb bypass using EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE after failed login attempts.
Run 2 did not identify path traversal via unsanitized config filename concatenation.
Run 2 identified buffer overflow in the expression parser with CWE-120 and quoted the unbounded strcpy into a fixed-size heap allocation.
Run 2 identified hardcoded admin credentials with CWE-798 and quoted the exact constant definition.
Run 2 did not identify the weak PRNG usage for session token generation.
Run 2 did not identify the thread-unsafe static timestamp cache in logging.
Run 2 did not identify insecure temporary file handling via system calls in audit logging.
Run 2 did not identify persistent authentication state across sessions.
Run 2 did not identify unhandled exceptions during variable parsing (std::stod).
Run 2 did not identify integer underflow in memory-pool block splitting.
Run 2 did not identify unbounded recursion in the expression parser.
Run 2 did not identify the data race in the result cache evaluation.
Run 2 did not identify the TOCTOU race condition in config file loading.
Run 2 did not identify integer division by zero in command routing.

buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|832|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }|input_buffer_ (1024 bytes) is overflowed by strcpy when input length is >= 1024.|entering an expression >= 1024 characters.|use std::string or bounded copy functions; ensure null terminator space.->buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|832|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }|input_buffer_ (1024 bytes) is overflowed by strcpy when input length is >= 1024.|entering an expression >= 1024 characters.|use std::string or bounded copy functions; ensure null terminator space.
